description: The Olympic swimming competition venue, a unique membrane structure building, now serves as the Ice Cube Ice Sports Center.
One of China's oldest surviving imperial gardens, with the White Pagoda as its iconic structure.
A commercial district with over 500 years of history, preserving the traditional Beijing hutong style and commercial culture.
The street where China's highest ancient educational institution was located, featuring well-preserved traditional architecture and a rich cultural atmosphere.
The Monument to the People's Heroes standing at the center of Tiananmen Square, a sacred memorial structure honoring revolutionary martyrs.
A famous hutong commercial street in Beijing, featuring unique shops and local delicacies, offering an experience of traditional courtyard culture.
The ruins of a Qing Dynasty imperial garden, a historical witness to the 'Garden of Gardens,' and a patriotic education base.
The main stadium of the 2008 Olympics, a masterpiece of modern architectural art and a symbol of Olympic spirit.
A distinctive artistic hutong in Beijing, home to creative shops and cafes, offering an experience of old Beijing hutong culture.
One of the earliest zoos opened in China, renowned for housing giant pandas and other rare animals.
The main venue area of the 2008 Olympics, home to iconic structures such as the Bird's Nest and the Water Cube.
The highest point of Beijing's central axis, the best location for a panoramic view of the Forbidden City, and a Ming Dynasty imperial garden.
An internationally renowned contemporary art district, a creative cultural park transformed from former industrial factories.
A cultural and leisure street in Beijing, featuring creative shops, cafes, and specialty restaurants.
A fashion landmark in Beijing, a hub for international brands and a center for modern urban nightlife.
One of China's top science and engineering universities, with a beautiful campus environment and a strong academic atmosphere.
